seoTitle: De Grey vs Deepdale — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between De Grey and Deepdale. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: De Grey vs Deepdale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in De Grey and Deepdale.
heading: De Grey vs Deepdale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between De Grey, Western Australia and Deepdale, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Deepdale has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to De Grey (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is De Grey or Deepdale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: De Grey has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Deepdale scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: De Grey scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Deepdale scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in De Grey or Deepdale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Deepdale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
